12	# Test Plan: Multi-Worker `ddx try` Reliability
13	
14	## Scope
15	
16	Validate that concurrent `ddx try` and `ddx work` executions in multiple
17	worktrees make progress without turning local git or tracker coordination into
18	the bottleneck. The suite is intentionally local-only: it uses fixture
19	repositories, the deterministic `script` harness, local clone/worktree attempt
20	backends, and subprocesses with isolated `HOME`/`XDG_DATA_HOME`. It must not
21	depend on network access, external model providers, Docker image pulls, hosted
22	git remotes, or developer-specific agent CLIs.
23	
24	This plan covers host-local concurrency for one project root. Multi-machine
25	coordination remains API-001/SD-020 scope; native toolchain cache contention
26	remains project policy per TD-010.
27	
28	## Contract
29	
30	Concurrent workers may serialize short parent-repo mutation windows. They must
31	not serialize the harness wait or the agent's work inside the attempt worktree.
32	
33	The bounded mutation windows are:
34	
35	1. pre-dispatch tracker commit, dirty-checkpoint, base resolution, and attempt
36	   workspace registration; slow clone/docker setup must not monopolize the
37	   main-git lock;
38	2. durable audit and evidence publication writes;
39	3. landing, preserve-ref creation, target ref update, and main-worktree index
40	   sync; post-land hooks and other arbitrary commands must not execute while the
41	   main-git lock is held;
42	4. startup cleanup mutation of stale execution worktrees and worker state.
43	
44	`index.lock` and `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` hold times are performance contracts,
45	not best-effort diagnostics. The default caps remain 10 s for `index.lock` and
46	30 s for `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`; fast tests should assert much smaller local
47	budgets where the fixture is deterministic.
48	
49	## Existing Coverage
50	
51	- `cli/internal/integration/lock_contention_test.go` proves 5
52	  `ddx work --watch` workers and 20 operator bead commands can overlap without
53	  operator tracker-lock timeouts, and asserts p99 lock holds stay below the
54	  configured caps.
55	- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_lock_scope_test.go` proves the git index lock
56	  and DDx tracker lock are not held across the harness subprocess wait.
57	- `cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap_test.go` proves default caps, cap override,
58	  violation logging, and `lock-violation.json` evidence.
59	- `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go` covers main-git lock sharing across
60	  linked worktrees, stale-lock recovery, malformed lock diagnostics, and retry
61	  policy.
62	- `cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go` and `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go` cover
63	  JSONL tracker concurrent append, update, claim, and close invariants.
64	
65	## Gaps And Required Tests
66	
67	### Fast Chaos
68	
69	- `TestChaos_PreDispatchMutationWindowDoesNotHoldLockAcrossHarnessWait`:
70	  instrument the pre-dispatch path with a script harness that sleeps after
71	  workspace preparation. Assert tracker-lock release occurs before the
72	  subprocess-running interval and that workspace creation is the last operation
73	  inside the lock.
74	- `TestChaos_DurableAuditCommitUnderWorkerAndOperatorContention`: run several
75	  local-clone `ddx try` attempts that all publish evidence while concurrent
76	  `ddx bead create/update/close` commands run. Assert no tracker-lock timeout,
77	  no missing `prompt.md`/`manifest.json`/`result.json`, and no `index.lock`
78	  failures in worker output.
79	- `TestChaos_StartupCleanupSkipsWhenAnotherWorkerOwnsCleanupLock`: start N
80	  `ddx work --once` processes against a fixture with stale worktree metadata.
81	  Assert exactly one cleanup pass mutates the stale worktree and the others emit
82	  `cleanup.skipped` without blocking claim.
83	- `TestChaos_PostLandCommandDoesNotHoldMainGitLock`: use a blocking post-land
84	  command runner or local script, then assert another goroutine can acquire the
85	  main-git lock while the post-land command is blocked.
86	- `TestChaos_AttemptPrepareDoesNotHoldMainGitLockForSlowClone`: inject a slow
87	  attempt backend or slow clone setup and assert another worker/operator can
88	  acquire `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` before the slow preparation unblocks.
89	
90	### Integration
91	
92	- `TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryDistinctBeads_LocalClone`: seed 8 independent
93	  beads, run 4 concurrent `ddx try <id>` subprocesses with the `script` harness
94	  and `--attempt-backend local-clone`, and assert all attempts either land or
95	  preserve cleanly with unique attempt IDs, unique worktree/clone paths, and no
96	  lingering attempt directories after cleanup.
97	- `TestIntegration_ConcurrentTrySameBead_OneClaimWins`: run 3 concurrent
98	  `ddx try <same-id>` subprocesses. Assert at most one attempt claims and runs;
99	  losing attempts exit through the existing not-claimable path without creating
100	  durable evidence bundles that look terminal.
101	- `TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryPreserveRefsUnique`: force non-landed attempts
102	  with `--no-merge` or a failing gate and assert hidden refs under
103	  `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/` are unique when attempts start within the
104	  same second.
105	
106	### Performance
107	
108	- `TestPerformance_PreDispatchMutationWindowP95UnderBudget`: measure
109	  tracker-lock hold duration for pre-dispatch with a warm local fixture. Target
110	  p95 < 2 s and max < 5 s for linked-worktree; record local-clone separately
111	  because clone checkout may be filesystem-sensitive.
112	- `TestPerformance_WorktreePrepareAndCleanupUnderBudget`: measure
113	  `git worktree add`/remove and local-clone prepare/cleanup for a small fixture.
114	  Target linked-worktree p95 < 2 s and cleanup p95 < 1 s; fail only on the
115	  deterministic fixture, not on large real repos.
116	- `TestPerformance_LockMetricsScenarioRunsUnderWallClockBudget`: keep the
117	  multi-worker contention scenario usable in CI by asserting the one-shot
118	  scenario completes under a bounded wall clock, with `go test -short` still
119	  skipping the subprocess-heavy variant.
120	- `TestPerformance_CheckoutSyncIndexRetryBudget`: exercise checkout sync under
121	  artificial `.git/index.lock` contention with a fakeable retry/backoff seam and
122	  assert the main-git lock hold stays below the deterministic budget.
123	
124	### Static/Contract Guards
125	
126	- `TestWorkerPathDoesNotUseFetchOriginAncestryCheck`: fail if `ddx try`,
127	  `ddx work`, or pre-claim worker paths wire `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` instead
128	  of the network-free local ancestry check.
129	- `TestManagedTrackerPathListsStayInSync`: assert the durable-audit managed
130	  path list, pre-claim tracker metadata list, and staged-path exemption helper
131	  classify `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`,
132	  `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, and `.ddx/attachments/...` identically.
133	- `TestWorkerFailurePathsReleaseClaimAtomically`: inject a heartbeat-removal
134	  failure and assert worker failure paths use `Release` or otherwise avoid a
135	  fresh sidecar lease that keeps an open bead invisible to `ReadyExecution`.
136	
137	## Fixture Rules
138	
139	- Use `testutils.BuildDDxBinary` for subprocess tests so spawned workers execute
140	  the code under test.
141	- Use the `script` harness only; directive files may `sleep-ms`, create files,
142	  write no-change rationale, or commit.
143	- Restrict subprocess environment to isolated `HOME`, isolated `XDG_DATA_HOME`,
144	  `GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null`, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, and a minimal `PATH`
145	  containing git and POSIX shell tools.
146	- Prefer a small fixture repo with 5-10 beads. Large-repo performance belongs in
147	  an optional benchmark, not a required guard.
148	- Record lock metrics from `.ddx/metrics/lock-events.jsonl`; assertions should
149	  use p95/p99 and max hold durations, plus explicit non-vacuity checks.
150	
151	## Exit Criteria
152	
153	- The fast chaos and performance tests run without network access and do not
154	  invoke external agent CLIs.
155	- Concurrent worker tests prove no lock is held across harness wait, no operator
156	  bead command fails with tracker-lock timeout, and no terminal attempt evidence
157	  is missing required bundle files.
158	- Worktree prepare/cleanup and pre-dispatch lock windows have numeric budgets
159	  that fail deterministically on the local fixture before they become user-facing
160	  multi-worker stalls.
161

1	// Package testutils provides shared helpers for tests and acceptance demos
2	// that need a clean ddx-initialized git repo without polluting the main DDx
3	// project. The canonical entry point is NewFixtureRepo, which wraps
4	// scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh and auto-cleans via t.Cleanup.
5	package testutils
6	
7	import (
8		"os"
9		"os/exec"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sync"
12		"testing"
13	)
14	
15	// fixtureRepoBaseName is the leaf directory name given to every fixture
16	// project. It must satisfy the bead-identifier charset (^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$,
17	// see internal/bead/id.go), because the bead prefix is derived from the git
18	// root's basename — a t.TempDir() leaf such as "001" would be numeric-only but
19	// the surrounding test-name path can contain characters (e.g. "_") that are
20	// rejected by `ddx bead create`. Nesting a fixed safe name under t.TempDir()
21	// guarantees a valid prefix regardless of the test name.
22	const fixtureRepoBaseName = "ddxfixture"
23	
24	// NewFixtureRepo builds a clean ddx-initialized git repo for the given profile
25	// (minimal, standard, multi-project, federated) and returns its path. The repo
26	// is created under t.TempDir() so it is removed automatically when the test
27	// finishes (the auto-clean promised in scripts/build-fixture-repo.md).
28	//
29	// For minimal/standard the returned path is the project root; for
30	// multi-project/federated it is the parent dir whose sub-projects live
31	// underneath (proj-a, proj-b / hub, spoke).
32	//
33	// The ddx binary used for seeding is resolved via DDxBinary. Global and system
34	// git config are neutralized for the seeding subprocess so a developer box's
35	// commit.gpgsign (which points at a host-specific signer) does not break the
36	// fixture's own commits; repo-local commit.gpgsign is additionally disabled on
37	// each created project so subsequent ddx commits in the test never attempt to
38	// sign.
39	func NewFixtureRepo(t *testing.T, profile string) string {
40		t.Helper()
41	
42		bin := DDxBinary(t)
43		dest := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), fixtureRepoBaseName)
44		script := filepath.Join(repoRoot(t), "scripts", "build-fixture-repo.sh")
45	
46		emptyGlobalCfg := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "gitconfig-global")
47		if err := os.WriteFile(emptyGlobalCfg, nil, 0o644); err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("write neutral global git config: %v", err)
49		}
50	
51		cmd := exec.Command("bash", script, dest, "--profile", profile)
52		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
53			"DDX_BIN="+bin,
54			"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL="+emptyGlobalCfg,
55			"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null",
56		)
57		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("build-fixture-repo.sh failed (profile=%s): %v\n%s", profile, err, out)
59		}
60	
61		for _, dir := range fixtureProjectDirs(dest, profile) {
62			gc := exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "commit.gpgsign", "false")
63			gc.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null")
64			if out, err := gc.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
65				t.Fatalf("disable commit.gpgsign in %s: %v\n%s", dir, err, out)
66			}
67		}
68	
69		return dest
70	}
71	
72	// fixtureProjectDirs returns the per-project git roots created for a profile,
73	// so repo-local config can be applied to each.
74	func fixtureProjectDirs(dest, profile string) []string {
75		switch profile {
76		case "multi-project":
77			return []string{filepath.Join(dest, "proj-a"), filepath.Join(dest, "proj-b")}
78		case "federated":
79			return []string{filepath.Join(dest, "hub"), filepath.Join(dest, "spoke")}
80		default:
81			return []string{dest}
82		}
83	}
84	
85	var (
86		builtBinaryOnce sync.Once
87		builtBinaryPath string
88		builtBinaryErr  error
89	)
90	
91	// DDxBinary resolves a ddx binary for fixture seeding and subprocess tests. It
92	// prefers $DDX_BIN, then `ddx` on PATH, and otherwise lazily builds one from
93	// cli/ once per process. The built binary lives in an os.MkdirTemp dir that
94	// outlives any single test so concurrent fixtures can share it.
95	func DDxBinary(t *testing.T) string {
96		t.Helper()
97		if env := os.Getenv("DDX_BIN"); env != "" {
98			if _, err := os.Stat(env); err == nil {
99				return env
100			}
101		}
102		if p, err := exec.LookPath("ddx"); err == nil {
103			return p
104		}
105		return BuildDDxBinary(t)
106	}
107	
108	// BuildDDxBinary builds the ddx binary from the current cli/ source tree once
109	// per process and returns its path. Tests that must exercise the current source
110	// (rather than a possibly-stale `ddx` on PATH) should build with this and pin it
111	// via t.Setenv("DDX_BIN", ...) so NewFixtureRepo seeds with the same binary. The
112	// binary lives in an os.MkdirTemp dir that outlives any single test.
113	func BuildDDxBinary(t *testing.T) string {
114		t.Helper()
115		builtBinaryOnce.Do(func() {
116			dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-fixture-bin-*")
117			if err != nil {
118				builtBinaryErr = err
119				return
120			}
121			out := filepath.Join(dir, "ddx")
122			build := exec.Command("go", "build", "-buildvcs=false", "-o", out, ".")
123			build.Dir = cliDir(t)
124			if combined, err := build.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
125				builtBinaryErr = err
126				t.Logf("go build ddx from source failed:\n%s", combined)
127				return
128			}
129			builtBinaryPath = out
130		})
131		if builtBinaryErr != nil {
132			t.Fatalf("build ddx binary from source: %v", builtBinaryErr)
133		}
134		return builtBinaryPath
135	}
136	
137	// cliDir walks up from the test's working directory to the directory that
138	// holds go.mod (the cli/ module root).
139	func cliDir(t *testing.T) string {
140		t.Helper()
141		wd, err := os.Getwd()
142		if err != nil {
143			t.Fatalf("getwd: %v", err)
144		}
145		dir := wd
146		for {
147			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "go.mod")); err == nil {
148				return dir
149			}
150			parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
151			if parent == dir {
152				t.Fatalf("could not locate go.mod (cli module root) from %s", wd)
153			}
154			dir = parent
155		}
156	}
157	
158	// repoRoot returns the repository root (parent of the cli/ module dir), where
159	// scripts/ lives.
160	func repoRoot(t *testing.T) string {
161		t.Helper()
162		return filepath.Dir(cliDir(t))
163	}

42	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {

51	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:

53	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
54	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
55	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time

12	// BuildLandingGateContext prepares the inputs the gate evaluator needs to
13	// score a worker's result before LandBeadResult decides merge-vs-preserve:
14	//
15	//  1. Pin res.ResultRev to refs/ddx/gate-pins/<beadID>/<attemptID>
16	//     so the SHA stays alive while the ephemeral worktree exists.
17	//  2. WorktreeAdd a temp worktree under the configured DDx execution scratch
18	//     root at the pinned ref so gate evaluators can read the
19	//     result tree without disturbing the main worktree.
20	//  3. Read the worker manifest from that result tree and extract governing IDs.
21	//     When the manifest declares no governing IDs the temporary worktree is
22	//     removed immediately and the caller skips gate evaluation.
23	//
24	// The returned cleanup closure removes the worktree and deletes the pin ref.
25	// Callers MUST defer cleanup() before returning to avoid orphaned worktrees.
26	//
27	// On any error the function attempts to roll back partial state (pin+remove)
28	// and returns ("", nil, noop, err).
29	func BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps GitOps) (wtPath string, governIDs []string, cleanup func(), err error) {
30		noop := func() {}
31	
32		if res == nil || res.ManifestFile == "" {
33			return "", nil, noop, nil
34		}
35		if res.ResultRev == "" {
36			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("BuildLandingGateContext: ResultRev empty, cannot pin")
37		}
38	
39		// Pin ResultRev so the worktree-add target stays alive even though the worker
40		// worktree was removed. Use bead+attempt to avoid collisions across attempts.
41		attemptKey := res.AttemptID
42		if attemptKey == "" {
43			attemptKey = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
44		}

48		}
49		unpin := func() { _ = gitOps.DeleteRef(projectRoot, pinRef) }

63		}

68			unpin()
69		}

72		if _, statErr := os.Stat(manifestPath); statErr != nil {

87	// EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical "run gate evaluation and
88	// write the bundle artifacts" sequence used by LandBeadResult and by upstream
89	// callers (server work) that want gate eval before submitting through
90	// the coordinator. It delegates to evaluateRequiredGates for the actual gate
91	// scoring, then mutates res to set GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
92	// RatchetEvidence, and RatchetSummary, and writes checks.json into the
93	// execution bundle. Returns (anyRequiredFailed, ratchetFailed, err).
94	//
95	// When wtPath is empty or no governing IDs are provided, gate evaluation is
96	// skipped and res.RequiredExecSummary is set to "skipped" (matching
97	// summarizeGates behavior). checksArtifactPath, when non-empty and gate
98	// results exist, is the absolute path checks.json is written to;
99	// checksArtifactRel is the bundle-relative path stored in res.ChecksFile on
100	// successful write.
101	func EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(wtPath string, governIDs []string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, projectRoot string, checksArtifactPath, checksArtifactRel string) (anyRequiredFailed bool, ratchetFailed bool, err error) {
102		if res == nil {
103			return false, false, fmt.Errorf("EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult: res is nil")
104		}
105	
106		var gateResults []GateCheckResult
107		var anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed bool
108		if wtPath != "" && len(governIDs) > 0 {
109			var gerr error
110			gateResults, anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gerr = evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath, governIDs)
111			if gerr != nil {
112				return false, false, fmt.Errorf("evaluating required gates: %w", gerr)
113			}
114		}
115	
116		res.GateResults = gateResults
117		res.RequiredExecSummary = summarizeGates(gateResults, anyGateFailed)
118		res.RatchetEvidence = collectRatchetEvidence(gateResults)
119		res.RatchetSummary = summarizeRatchets(res.RatchetEvidence)
120	
121		// Write checks.json when gate evaluation produced results and a path was
122		// provided. Best-effort: a write failure leaves res.ChecksFile empty but
123		// does not abort the gate evaluation.
124		if len(gateResults) > 0 && checksArtifactPath != "" {
125			checks := executeBeadChecks{
126				AttemptID:       res.AttemptID,
127				EvaluatedAt:     time.Now().UTC(),
128				Summary:         res.RequiredExecSummary,
129				Results:         gateResults,
130				RatchetSummary:  res.RatchetSummary,
131				RatchetEvidence: res.RatchetEvidence,
132			}
133			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(checksArtifactPath, checks); writeErr == nil {
134				res.ChecksFile = checksArtifactRel
135			}
136		}
137	
138		return anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, nil
139	}

data.text: Now I understand the full picture. `LandConflictAutoRecover` uses `git merge -s ort -X ours` which resolves ALL textual conflicts, so same-bead sequential attempts always close the bead. The fix is to redesign AC3 to use two concurrent attempts on two different beads — both hit conflicts, both create preserve refs, refs are verified unique. This still proves the 4-byte-random uniqueness guarantee.
